MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER INTERROGATORIES, PROOF OF SERVICE PLNTF'S NTC OF CHANGED DATE REGARDING MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES; SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES; REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION & REQUEST FOR ADMISSIONS & SANCTIONS FILED BY PLAINTIFF CRESPIN, EDITH MARCELA HEARING SET FOR AUG-30-2004 AT 09:00 AM IN DEPT 612

DISCOVERY 612, MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER INTERROGATORIES - APPEARANCES: MICHAEL KOWALKSI, ESQ., (415) 464-8885 FOR DEFENDANT/OPPOSING PARTY, DR. PAVEL NIDERMAN. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO DOCUMENT DEMAND NO. 4. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO DOCUMENT DEMAND NOS. 8 AND 9, 11. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO DOCUMENT DEMAND NOS. 10, 11, 12, 13, 14, 15 LIMITED TO EMPLOYEES. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO DOCUMENTE DEMAND NOS. 18, 19, 20, 21, 22, AND 23. COPIES SHALL BE PRODUCED. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO DOCUMENT DEMAND NO. 30. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES NOS. 1, 2, AND 3. THE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. THE RESPONSE IS INADEQUATE. THE MOTION IS GRANTED AS TO THE REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS. THE OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED. THE RESPONSES ARE INADEQUATE. THE MOTION IS DENIED AS TO THE FORM INTERROGATORIES. THE OBJECTIONS ARE SUSTAINED. THE REMEDY WAS TO COMPEL RESPONSES AS TO THE RESPONSES. FURTHER RESPONSES SHALL BE MADE WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE OPPOSITION AS TO THE DOCUMENT DEMANDS, SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, AND REQUESTS FOR ADMISSIONS WAS WITHOUT SUBSTANTIAL JUSTIFICATION. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED AGAINST DEFENDANT IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,325, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. PROD 17-GRANTED. FORM INTERROG. 17.1-RESPOND-MOTION GRANTED. SANCTIONS GRANTED AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND HIS COUNSEL JOINTLY. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
DISMISSAL OF ENTIRE ACTION OF ALL PARTIES AND ALL CAUSES OF ACTION WITH PREJUDICE
